title: quercus garryana
intro: Quercus garryana, also known as the Garry oak, is a deciduous tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, characterized by its broad, rounded crown and lobed leaves. The medicinal properties of Quercus garryana lie in its bark, which contains tannins and phenolic compounds with astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Quercus garryana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Quercus garryana can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, often used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems.
